coredata.dc:title: Flexible and washable CDs@Eu-MOFs/PVDF multifunctional thin films as highly selective sensing for nitrobenzene and 4-Nitrophenol
coredata.dc:creator: Wang, Xinlin
Zhu, Rudong
Wang, Xiangkui
Liu, Fengjiao
Gao, Yan
Guan, Rongfeng
Chen, Yalei
coredata.dc:description: In this paper, the application of metal organic frameworks (MOFs) doped with carbon dots in fluorescence detection was studied.CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF thin films were prepared by simple solvothermal method, carbon dots modification and in-situ growth process.The structure and morphology of the film was characterized by XRD, SEM, FT-IR and XPS.The results show that CDs@Eu-MOFs membrane has strong red fluorescence, good stability in methanol solvent, and high selectivity and sensitivity to nitrobenzene and 4-nitrophenol.The CDs@Eu-MOFs/PVDF film material can be used to detect nitrobenzene and 4-nitrophenol in methanol solvent and gaseous state. The detection limits of nitrobenzene and 4-nitrophenol in liquid are 0.2807 mg/L and 0.0168 mg/L, and the detection limits of nitrobenzene and 4-nitrophenol in gaseous state are 0.346 mg/L and 0.0136 mg/L.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Eu-MOF/PVDF Film
CDs
Detection of nitrobenzene
Detection of 4-nitrophenol

xocs:meta.xocs:articleinfo: absattachment articleinfo articlenumber articletitlenorm authfirstinitialnorm authfirstsurnamenorm cid cids contenttype copyright crossmark dateloaded dateloadedtxt datesearch datesort dateupdated dco docsubtype doctype doi eid ewtransactionid hubeid indexeddate issn issnnorm itemstage itemtransactionid itemweight openaccess openarchive pg pgfirst pii piinorm pubdateend pubdatestart pubdatetxt pubyr sectiontitle sortorder srctitle srctitlenorm srctype ssids alllist content subj subheadings suppl tomb volfirst volissue volumelist webpdf webpdfpagecount yearnav figure table e-component body acknowledge affil appendices articletitle auth authfirstini authfull authkeywords authlast grantnumber grantsponsor grantsponsorid highlightsabst primabst pubtype ref specialabst

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Sensing of liquid and gaseous NB

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: The schematic for the CDs and CDs@Eu-MOF film synthesis route.
SEM images of Eu-MOF(a),CDs@Eu-MOF(b),PVDF Film (c) and CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF Film (d).TEM images of CDs@Eu-MOF（e and f）.
(a) (b) distribution of CDs at different ratios; (c) Local amplification of CDs(TEM).
SEM images of Eu-MOF(a, b) and EDS mappings of the C, O, N and Eu elements in the Eu-MOF sample(b).
SEM images of CD@Eu-MOF(a, b) and EDS mappings of the C, O, N and Eu elements in the CD@Eu-MOF sample(b).

FTIR spectra of Eu-MOFs(black line), CDs@Eu-MOF(red line), PVDF Film(blue line) and CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF Film (green line). (For interpretation of the references to colour in this figure legend, the reader is referred to the web version of this article.)
(a) XPS spectra of Eu-MOFs,CDs@Eu-MOFs,CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF Film; (b) C 1 s; (c) O 1 s; (d) Eu3d.

The excitation (black line) and emission (red line) spectra of CDs@Eu-MOF Film. (the corresponding luminescence picture under 365 nm UV light irradiation is shown in the inset.). (For interpretation of the references to colour in this figure legend, the reader is referred to the web version of this article.)

(a)Emission spectra of CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF films in aqueous solution for 10 days, and the inset is the corresponding relative intensity at λem = 615 nm,(b)XRD spectra of CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF Films before and after soaking in methanol.

(a) the intensities of CDs@Eu-MOF (the 5D0→7F2 transition) dispersed in different organic solvents.The inserted photo was taken by CDs@Eu-MOF under a 365 nm UV lamp in different solvents (b)the intensities of CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF Film (the 5D0→7F2 transition) immersed in different organic solvents.The inserted photo was taken by CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF Film under a 365 nm UV lamp in different solvents.

(a) The fluorescence response of CDs@Eu-MOF in different concentrations of nitrobenzene solution (0 ∼ 2000 mg/L) (b) Relationship betweenthe concentration of NB and I0 / I. (0–2000 mg/L); (c) The concentration of NB and the I0/I linear fitting curve (0–200 mg/L).

(a) The fluorescence response of CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF Film in different concentrations of nitrobenzene solution (0–2000 mg/L); (b)Relationship betweenthe concentration of NB and I0 / I (0–2000 mg/L);(c) The concentration of NB and the I0/I linear fitting curve (0–300 mg/L).

(a) The fluorescence response of CDs@Eu-MOF-PVDF Film in different concentrations of NB gas(0–2000 mg/L);(b) Relationship betweenthe concentration of NB gas and I0 / I (0–2000 mg/L);(c) The concentration of NB gas and the I0/I linear fitting curve (0–400 mg/L).

(a) The fluorescence response of CDs@Eu-MOF in different concentrations of 4-NP solution (0–100 mg/L); (b) Relationship betweenthe concentration of 4-NP and I0/I(0–100 mg/L);(c) The concentration of 4-NP and the I0/I linear fitting curve (0–16 mg/L).

(a) The fluorescence response of CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF film in different concentrations of 4-NP solution (0–100 mg/L); (b)Relationship betweenthe concentration of 4-NP and I0/I (0–100 mg/L);(c) The concentration of 4-NP and the I0/I linear fitting curve (0–16 mg/L).

(a)The fluorescence response of CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF Film in different concentrations of 4-NP gas(0–100 mg/L); (b)Relationship betweenthe concentration of 4-NP gas and I0/I(0–100 mg/L)(c) The concentration of 4-NP gas and the I0/I linear fitting curve (0–40 mg/L).
HOMO and LUMO energy levels of MeOH,EtOH, NB,4-NP,1,10-phen and H4btec.
Ksc, R2 and DL of NB and 4-NP Detection with Different Materials(*LiquidTest, **GasTest).

head.ce:title: Flexible and washable CDs@Eu-MOFs/PVDF multifunctional thin films as highly selective sensing for nitrobenzene and 4-Nitrophenol

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Key Laboratory for Advanced Technology in Environmental Protection of Jiangsu Province, Yancheng Institute of Technology, Jiangsu, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Key Laboratory for Advanced Technology in Environmental Protection of Jiangsu Province

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Key Laboratory for Advanced Technology in Environmental Protection of Jiangsu Province, Yancheng Institute of Technology, Jiangsu, China

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: College of Mechanical Engineering, Yancheng Institute of Technology, Yancheng 224051, China
ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: College of Mechanical Engineering, Yancheng Institute of Technology, Yancheng 224051, China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: In this paper, the application of metal organic frameworks (MOFs) doped with carbon dots in fluorescence detection was studied.CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF thin films were prepared by simple solvothermal method, carbon dots modification and in-situ growth process.The structure and morphology of the film was characterized by XRD, SEM, FT-IR and XPS.The results show that CDs@Eu-MOFs membrane has strong red fluorescence, good stability in methanol solvent, and high selectivity and sensitivity to nitrobenzene and 4-nitrophenol.The CDs@Eu-MOFs/PVDF film material can be used to detect nitrobenzene and 4-nitrophenol in methanol solvent and gaseous state. The detection limits of nitrobenzene and 4-nitrophenol in liquid are 0.2807 mg/L and 0.0168 mg/L, and the detection limits of nitrobenzene and 4-nitrophenol in gaseous state are 0.346 mg/L and 0.0136 mg/L.

ce:data-availability.ce:para: Data will be made available on request.

ce:section.ce:para: Nitrobenzene (NB) is usually applied in medicine, pesticides, fuels, explosives, textiles, dyes and other fields as an important chemical raw material and chemical intermediate[1,2]. Nitrobenzene is non-degradable, highly toxic and carcinogenic, and its treatment has attracted increasing attention [3].At present, the United States, the European Union and China have listed nitrobenzene as a priority control pollutants.NB has also been identified by the US Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) as one of the world's most dangerous pollutants[4].Its toxicity is 20 ∼ 30 times that of common compounds, with typical carcinogenic, teratogenic, mutagenic and genotoxic, can cause great harm to human body[5–7].In addition, 4-nitrophenol (4-NP) is frequently employed in dyes,pesticides, leather industries and explosives[8,9].Another prevalent chemical pollutant is 4-NP, which can cause a number of health issues, including visceral damage, indigestion, heart injury, skin aging, dyspnea, and musculoskeletal degeneration [10].Therefore,4-NP and NB are listed as priority pollutants[11].At present, the detection methods of these two pollutants mainly include infrared and Raman spectroscopy[12], X-ray imaging[13], ionization spectroscopy (IMS)[14], electrochemical analysis[15] and chromatography-mass spectrometry[16],etc.Most of these methods are expensive equipment, complex operation, long measurement cycle, high cost and low efficiency. Therefore, there is need to develop high-sensitivity, low-cost and portable devices to detect NB and 4-NP. With the development of fluorescence sensor technology, fluorescent probes show the characteristics of simple, rapid and high sensitivity, and are widely used in food detection, drug analysis, environmental monitoring and bioassay[17–20].What's more, a compact, portable system for mobile monitoring can be created using fluorescence-based instruments.[21,22].

Due to their high porosity, huge specific surface area, and good expansibility, metal–organic frameworks (MOFs) made of metal ions and organic connectors have drawn a lot of interest[23–25].MOFs materials have been widely true to gas separation [26], catalysis[27], electrochemistry[28], drug delivery [29], etc., especially in the field of luminescent materials, they have a greater application prospect[30,31].At present, Ln-MOFs have been reported in selective sensing of various analytes, gas detection, biomolecule detection, and nitroaromatic compounds.Its superior fluorescence performance is due to the characteristic oxidation state valence state of rare earth ions is basically positive trivalent, with 4f electrons and 4f sub-layer of the seven filled electron orbits, the transition within the 4f configuration can produce fluorescence spectrum[23].In addition, the wide application of carbon dots (CDs) has attracted more and more attention. CDs have been widely used in sensors, photocatalysis, fluorescence bioimaging, drug delivery and optical electronics. Many ions have fluorescence quenching effect on CDs, so CDs have become one of the most developed fluorescent probes for detecting different anions and cations[32,33].In the realm of fluorescence sensing, CDs doped Eu-MOFs have been reported as of late, but the quantity is still quite limited. [34]. After doping Ln-MOFs with CDs, MOFs produce more photoluminescence electrons and holes and have more active sites[35,36].Therefore, we believe that more research results of CDs doped Ln-MOFs will be applied to environmental detection in the future.

Based on the above considerations, we synthesized Eu-MOF/PVDF film with carbon dots by a simple hydrothermal method.The specific scheme is to use 1,2,4,5-benzenetetracarboxylic acid(H4btec) and 1,10-phenanthro line monohydrate(1,10-phen) as ligands, Eu3+ as the metal skeleton [37], adding a certain amount of carbon dots[38],A novel fluorescent film probe (CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF film) was successfully prepared on PVDF film by in-situ growth method[39].The fluorescent probe was used for the purpose of NB and 4-NP detection, showing excellent stability, high selectivity and sensitivity. It is a promising bifunctional luminescent sensor material.

All the chemical reagents were available from commercial sources without purifying treatment. Eu(NO3)3·6H2O,1,10-phen,H4btec,Methanol(MeOH),ethanol(EtOH),tetrahydrofuran(THF),propan-2-ol(IPA),N,N-dimethylformamide(DMF),benzene,formaldehyde,N,N-dimethylacetamide(DMA),toluene, acetone were purchased from Aladdin Industrial Corporation, China. NaOH,nitrobenzene,p-Nitrophenol were purchased from Shanghai Macklin Biochemical Co.Polyvinylidene fluoride（PVDF）film were purchased from Haiyan New Oriental Plastic Technology Co.

The prepared powder and film samples were tested by X-ray diffraction (XRD) using a Ka-ray diffractometer (X'Pert3Powder). The morphology of the samples was analyzed by scanning electron microscopy (Nova NanoSEM 450) and transmission electron microscopy (JEM-2100F), and elemental analysis was performed using energy dispersive X-ray spectroscopy (EDS).A Fourier transform infrared spectrometer(FTIR, NEXUS-670, Nicolet) was used to assess the organic functional groups in the sample's infrared absorption properties.Determination of Elemental Composition and Chemical Valence of Samples by X-ray Photoelectron Spectroscopy (XPS, Thermo Escalab 250 Xi). The fluorescence properties of the samples were tested by fluorescence spectrophotometer (Agilent Cary Eclipse) at 320 nm excitation wavelength.UV–vis diffuse reflectance spectroscopy (UV–vis) UV absorption spectra of samples were measured using a dual-speed UV–vis spectrophotometer (Shimadzu UV2600, Japan). The wavelength range of the test was 300 nm ∼ 800 nm, and the scanning interval was 0.5 nm.

Preparation of CDs[40]. The 6 g glucose and 54 ml deionized water were poured into a 100 ml beaker and stirred at room temperature for 30 min. The glucose solution is then transferred to a 100 ml polytetrafluoroethylene lined stainless steel autoclave and held at 160 °C in an electrothermal blast oven for 3 h.After the reactor was cooled to room temperature, the resulting CDs solution was centrifuged. The supernatant was then taken and added to a 3500D dialysis bag for 12 h of purification. Centrifuge again, and finally configure the centrifuged supernatant into 80 ml of CDs solution into the refrigerator refrigerator for subsequent use.

Eu-MOF was prepared by hydrothermal method[34]. Specifically, Eu (NO3)3·6H2O (0.5 mmol, 0.223 g), 1,10-phen (0.5 mmol, 0.09 g), H4btec (0.5 mmol, 0.127 g), and NaOH (0.1 mmol, 0.04 g) were dissolved in 10 ml deionized water and 10 ml methanol.After around 30 min of stirring, the reaction mixture was placed into a stainless steel reactor with a Teflon liner and heated at 140 °C for 48 h. The obtained white powder was washed three times with deionized water and dried under vacuum at 70 °C to obtain Eu-MOF.

Preparation of CDs@Eu-MOF. Based on the above preparation process of Eu-MOF,CDs were added at the same time when configuring the reaction mixture, and the addition amounts were 1 ml, 3 ml, 5 ml, and 7 ml, respectively. The other reaction conditions are the same as the above steps of preparing Eu-MOF, and the obtained CDs@Eu-MOF are recorded as CDs@Eu-MOF-0,CDs@Eu-MOF-1, CDs @ Eu-MOF-3, CDs @ Eu-MOF-5, CDs @ Eu-MOF-7, respectively.

The process of preparing CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF Film is the same as that of preparing CDs @Eu-MOF, but the difference is that when the reaction solution is added to the reactor, a 1 × 3 cm3 PVDF film should be taken and put into the Teflon-lined stainless steel reactor at the same time. The remaining conditions remain unchanged. The amount of carbon dots is 3 ml.The obtained film was washed three times with water and dried under vacuum at 70 °C to obtain CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF film. Fig. 1 shows the sample preparation process.

We used the prepared materials for fluorescence detection of small organic molecules. In order to detect small organic molecules with powder, the prepared CDs@Eu-MOF (2 mg) was placed in various 2 ml organic solvents to make it evenly distributed by ultrasound, including IPA, THF, benzene, DMA, EtOH, MeOH, formaldehyde, DMF, H2O, acetone, NB and 4-NP. Fluorescence is then measured using a fluorescence spectrometer (Agilent Cary Eclipse). In order to use the film to detect small organic molecules, we attached the CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF film to the plexiglass and fixed it in a cuvette, adding different small organic molecules to be detected.Then the fluorescence of the film was detected by fluorescence spectrometer.

CDs@Eu-MOF was synthesized from Eu(NO3)3·6H2O, 1,10-phen, H4btec and carbon dots in methanol aqueous solution. The asymmetric unit consisted of one Eu (III) ion, one 1,10-phen ligand, two H4btec ligands and one coordinated water molecule. As shown in Fig.S1，the Eu(III) is surrounded by two N atoms of 1,10-phen ligand, two carboxyl O atoms from H4btec ligand and one carboxyl O atom from water atom, where the carbon dots are encapsulated in Eu-MOF. The XRD of the experimental samples' CDs@Eu-MOF-Film, CDs@Eu-MOF, Eu-MOF, and PVDF Film is displayed in Fig. 2 . Because of the small size of the CDs and their embedding into the MOF, XRD reveals that the diffraction peaks of Eu-MOF and CDs@Eu-MOF are essentially the same, indicating that the composite of CDs has no impact on the crystal structure of Eu-MOF[35].Comparing the diffraction peaks of CDs @ Eu-MOFs, CDs @ Eu-MOF film and PVDF film samples, the diffraction peaks of PVDF film at 2θ = 17.8°, 22.9°, 26.1°and CDs@Eu-MOF at 2θ = 10.1°, 16.3°, 29.9° also exist in the diffraction peaks of CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF Film, which indicates that CDs@Eu-MOFs are successfully grown on PVDF-Film[41].

Fig. 7 displays the prepared sample's FTIR spectrum,and the infrared spectrum shows that CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF Film have been successfully prepared. Both the Eu-MOF and CDs@Eu-MOF infrared spectra are consistent.Both Eu-MOF and CDs@Eu-MOF have obvious hydrogen bond OH stretching vibration at 3460 cm−1 [46].The asymmetric stretching vibrations of carboxylic acid groups are represented by the bands of Eu-MOF at 1600 cm−1 and 1370 cm−1, respectively[37].The highest stretching vibration of CH2 is at 1400 cm-1 in the infrared image of pure PVDF film[47], the peak stretching vibration of -CF2 is at 1210 cm−1 [48], and the peak stretching vibration of C-F is at 882 cm−1 [49].The synthesized CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF Film not only preserve the characteristic peaks of PVDF pure membrane at 882 cm−1,1210 cm−1 and 1400 cm−1, but also exist at 1600 cm−1 and 3460 cm−1, which are the characteristic peaks contained in Eu-MOF. These results show that the synthesis of CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF Film is successful. However, due to the high intensity of many peaks and many characteristic peaks of PVDF pure membrane, the stretching vibration peaks of hydrogen bonds and carboxylic acid groups are relatively small [50].

The elemental valence states of Eu-MOF, CDs@Eu-MOF and CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF Film samples (Fig. 8 a) were further studied by XPS, and their high-resolution spectra were analyzed. The image shows that the Eu, O, N, and C on the surfaces of CDs@Eu-MOFs and Eu-MOFs are identical.CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF Film has Eu, O, N and C as well as F element. This indicates that CDs@Eu-MOF was successfully grown on PVDF film, confirming the successful synthesis of CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF Film[51]. A high-resolution scanning image of C is shown in Fig. 8b..There are CO, CO, and CC bonds in each of the three samples, and their respective binding energies are 288.4, 285.1, and 284.5 ev. The CO binding energy of CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF film is reduced by 0.3 eV from 288.4 ev [52]. Fig. 8c is a high-precision scanning image of O1s. All three contain O2− and carboxyl groups with binding energies of 532.1 and 531.3 ev, respectively. The binding energy of O2− of CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF Film is 530.7 ev. Fig. 8d shows that the Eu valence states of the prepared materials are mainly Eu3+ (Eu3d3/2 and Eu3d5/2) [53].

The fluorescence spectra of Eu-MOF powder and CDs@Eu-MOF powder measured at an excitation wavelength of 320 nm are shown in Fig.S3. The intensity of Eu-MOF at 615 nm first rises and then falls with an increase in carbon dots with an increase in the doping concentration of carbon dots. The fluorescence intensity of Eu-MOF at 615 nm reaches its peak when the amount of carbon dots doped reaches 3 ml, after which it starts to decline.After adding carbon dots, the fluorescence intensity will increase. This is because after adding carbon dots, under the excitation of 320 nm, the energy of the original ligand is not only directly transferred to Eu3+, but also transferred to the carbon dots. As shown in Fig S4,the UV spectrum of CDs,1,10-phen and H4btec represents that the absorption spectrum of the CDs overlaps with the one between the ligands, so that the energy transfer can occur between the CDs and the ligands. In Fig S5, due to the partial electron transfer from the ligand to CDs, the intensity of the 420 nm-490 nm, luminescence band decreases,the emission intensity at 615 nm is enhanced due to the transfer of photoelectrons captured by CDs and electrons transferred from ligands to the excited state of Eu3+. However, when the concentration of CDs is too high, the emission intensity at 615 nm decreases due to concentration quenching. Therefore, we chose to doped 3 ml carbon dots to modify Eu-MOF.

Fig. 9 displays the CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF film's excitation and emission spectra.A CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF film exhibits the typical emission peaks of Eu3+ at = 543,591,615,650, and 700 nm when the excitation wavelength is 320 nm. These emission peaks are caused by the typical f-f transitions of Eu3+ ions, which emit 5D0→7F0、5D0→7F1、5D0→7F2、5D0→7F3 and 5D0→7F4, respectively. Among these emission peaks, the typical electric dipole transition of 5D0→7F2 has the strongest red emission peak at 615 nm. In addition, the CD@Eu-MOF/PVDF film exhibits strong red light under 365 nm UV irradiation, which can be easily observed by the naked eye, indicating the presence of the antenna. The large chromophore formed between Eu3+ ion and ligands acts as an ' antenna ', and the energy is transmitted from the ligand to the metal through the antenna for the enhancement of luminescence intensity.

In order to further study the fluorescence properties and stability of CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF Film in aqueous solution.We measured the fluorescence of CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF Film in aqueous solution with 320 nm excitation. The outcomes are displayed in Fig. 10 a CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF Film shows the characteristic fluorescence of Eu3+,and also shows its structural stability and fluorescence stability. The film material is immersed in water at normal temperature for ten days. The fluorescence intensity also maintains sufficient stability, which indicates that the film has good structural stability in water and can maintain strong red fluorescence. As shown in Fig. 10b,the XRD of the CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF Films soaked in methanol and found that they were almost identical and unchanged, indicating the stability of the CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF Films structure.Additionally, it demonstrates the utility of the manufactured CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF film as a fluorescence probe in aqueous media.(Fig S6 and S7 show the fluorescence stability of Eu-MOF and CDs@Eu-MOF).As shown in Fig.S8,after the CDs@Eu-MOF and CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF Film were tested for different concentrations of NB, the fluorescence detection was performed after washing with deionized water and ethanol to determine the reusability of the CDs@Eu-MOF and CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF Film.

Selective experiments of CDs@Eu-MOF and CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF film: To examine the potential of CDs@Eu-MOF for the detection of organic small molecules, we chose a number of organic solvent small molecules.The fluorescence intensity of the powder MOFs in the organic solution is shown in Fig. 11 a.In different organic solvents, Eu3+ ions exhibit different luminescence intensities of 5D0→7F2 transition (IPA > THF > benzene > DMA > EtOH > MeOH > formaldehyde > DMF > H2O > acetone > NB > 4-NP). Taking the same reagent, the selectivity of the film was tested. Fig. 11b shows that the selectivity exhibited by the film is consistent with the selectivity result of the powder.This indicates that CDs@Eu-MOF and CDs@Eu-MOF-PVDF films can be used to detect NB and 4-NP. At the same time, because the material has good luminescence in MeOH, NB and 4-NP can be dissolved in MeOH.Considering the safety, we choose MeOH as the solvent to detect NB and 4-NP.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Sensing of liquid and gaseous NB
ce:section.ce:para: When CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF Film was used to detect NB solution, In the range of 0–300 mg/L, the fluorescence intensity showed a good linear relationship with the concentration of NB solution(R2 = 0.995), and the Ksv was calculated to be 2.03 × 103M−1.The fluorescence detection limit of CDs@Eu-MOF-PVDF Film for NB was 0.2807 mg/L based on the DL = 3Sb/K equation.

We not only used CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF Film to detect NB in different concentrations of aqueous solution, we also used the film to detect the fluorescence intensity at different concentrations of NB gaseous state. As shown in Fig. 14 c, when the NB solution was detected by CDs@Eu-MOF-PVDF Film, there was an excellent linear connection between the fluorescence intensity and the concentration in the range of 0–400 mg/L (R2 = 0.964), and the calculated Ksv was 1.54 × 103 M−1. The fluorescence detection limit of CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF Film for NB gas was 0.364 mg/L.

Figs. 15 and 16
depict the experimentally determined connection between the concentration of 4-NP and the luminescence intensity of CDs@Eu-MOF and CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF Film, respectively.Fig. 15a makes it very evident that as the concentration of 4-NP rises, the fluorescence intensity of Eu3+ at 615 nm falls.When the concentration of 4-NP reaches 100 mg/L, nearly 97 % of fluorescence is quenched.According to the Stern-Volmer curve, the fluorescence intensity demonstrated an excellent linear relationship with the concentration of 4-NP in the range of 0–16 mg/L.(R2 = 0.996), and the Ksv was calculated to be 6.051 × 104M−1. Based on the DL = 3Sb / K equation, the fluorescence DL of CDs@ Eu-MOF for 4-NP is 0.00929 mg/L.

As shown in Fig. 16c, when CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF Film was used for detection. In the range of 0–30 mg/L, the fluorescence intensity displayed a strong linear relationship with the concentration of 4-NP solution(R2 = 0.995), and the Ksv was calculated to be 3.382 × 104M−1. Based on the DL = 3Sb / K equation, the fluorescence DL of CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF Film for 4-NP was 0.0168 mg/L.

As shown in Fig. 17 c, CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF Film was used to detect 4-NP gas phase. In the range of 0–40 mg/L,the fluorescence intensity shows a good linear relationship with 4-NP gas concentration.(R2 = 0.976), and the Ksv was calculated to be 4.194 × 104M−1. Based on the DL = 3Sb / K equation, the fluorescence DL of CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF Film for 4-NP gas was 0.0136 pm.

It can be seen from Table 1 that the detection effect of CDs@Eu-MOF composited with carbon dots is significantly better than that of Eu-MOF. After the film is prepared, although the Ksv decreases, it has little effect on its detection performance. Compared with powder, it is more convenient and simple to detect pollutants with thin films, and it can be used to detect pollutant gases, which is an advantage that powder does not have.Comparing with the recent results of similar literature, Liu[54] used luminescent lanthanide MOF for nitrobenzene with a range of 0–100 mg/L and DL of 0.0166 mg/L. Li [55]used Zn-MOF to detect 4-NP, with a range of 0–1 mg/L and DL of 0.28 mg/L. The detection range of this literature is wider than them, and the use of thin film detection is an advantage they do not have.

According to the reports, the quenching mechanism of MOF fluorescence by small organic molecules is currently incomplete.Therefore, we use Gaussian 09 package to calculate the molecular orbitals of MeOH, EtOH, NB, 4-NP, 1,10-phen and H4btec using B3LYP and 6-311G basis sets (Fig. 18 ). By utilizing the photoinduced electron transfer (PET) mechanism, the findings in Fig. 18 can effectively explain the quenching mechanism[56,57]. Firstly, the transport of electrons from the donor Eu-MOF to the acceptor NB and 4-NP will cause fluorescence quenching because of the high electron deficiency of NB and 4-NP and the porosity of the framework. Secondly, the energy bands of MOFs made from d10 metal ions with highly localized electronic states are typically narrow,so large “molecular” and molecular orbital (MOs) energy levels, respectively, can be estimated for infinitely extended MOFs and their valence band (VB) and conduction band (CB) energy levels[58].The CB energy level of MOFs is typically greater than nitro compounds' lowest unoccupied molecular orbitals (LUMOs).The lowest unoccupied molecular orbital (LUMO) levels of NB (-2.44 eV) and 4-NP (-2.73 eV) are lower than those of MeOH (2.10 eV) and EtOH (2.10 eV), and lower than those of the two ligands 1,10-phen (-1.67 eV) and H4btec (-1.82 eV). Under certain excitation, electrons are transported to the LUMO of NB and 4-NP via two ligands, changing the distribution of the electrons and quenching the fluorescence.

In this study, Eu-MOF/PVDF films doped with carbon dots were prepared under simple and mild conditions. After the composite carbon dots Eu-MOF, in addition to its own fluorescence intensity enhancement, in the detection of sensitivity, stability and reusability have been significantly improved. Through various reaction mechanisms, CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF film can be transformed into extremely sensitive and selective fluorescent probes for NB and 4-NP, according to studies on its luminescence properties. At the same time compared to powder, film stability and portability are much higher than the powder. Therefore, the prepared CDs@Eu-MOF/PVDF film has important application prospects for the detection of NB and 4-NP.

Xinlin Wang: Investigation, Validation, Data curation, Writing – original draft, Writing – review & editing. Rudong Zhu: Methodology, Software. Xiangkui Wang: Methodology. Fengjiao Liu: Software. Yan Gao: Methodology. Rongfeng Guan: Supervision, Writing – review & editing, Funding acquisition. Yalei Chen: Supervision, Methodology.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This article was supported by the National Natural Science Foundation of China (21878257, 21276220), Major Research & Developement Projects (Social Development) of Jiangsu Province (BE2020671), High Technology R & D demonstration project, Department of Emergency Management, Jiangsu Province (YJGL-YF-2020-4), Jiangsu Building Materials and Environmental Protection Equipment Collaborative Innovation Project (CP201502),Postgraduate Research & Practice Innovation Program of Yancheng Institute of Technology(SJCX21_XZ007), thanks to the Analysis and Testing Center of Yancheng Institute of Technology for its support in sample characterization.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data to this article can be found online at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.inoche.2023.110423.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para:
The following are the Supplementary data to this article:
Supplementary data 1
